WRITING RULES
‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ

1. TITLE
   ‚Ä¢ ALL CAPS or wrapped in "quotation marks"
   ‚Ä¢ Maximum 10 words
   ‚Ä¢ Must stop the scroll ‚Äî punchy, contrarian, or curiosity-inducing

2. BULLET POINTS
   ‚Ä¢ Use 3 to 5 bullets only
   ‚Ä¢ Each bullet = one sharp idea, not a sentence
   ‚Ä¢ Read like a master's private notebook ‚Äî aphoristic, not explanatory

3. VISUAL RHYTHM
   ‚Ä¢ Keep every line extremely short (ideally under 10 words)
   ‚Ä¢ Heavy white space between lines
   ‚Ä¢ NO paragraphs. NO filler words.
   ‚Ä¢ Write like you're carving stone, not typing prose

4. CLOSING LINE
   ‚Ä¢ One final standalone sentence
   ‚Ä¢ Must hit like a punchline or a verdict
   ‚Ä¢ Should feel quotable on its own
